mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained aggregate supply and rising input costs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not predict negative SPX returns, with forward return mean statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate inflation fears and expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerate signals unsustainable consumer leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative SPX returns, with 20-day forward returns averaging positive values in > 55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapidly expanding corporate output capacity and margin improvement, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in recovery regimes does not yield positive 5-day SPX returns, with the signal Sharpe ratio falling below 0.5 over any 2-year evaluation window.
